channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Professional wrestler Joe Doering dies at 44 after long battle with brain cancer
item.description: Professional wrestler Joe Doering has died at the age of 44 following a prolonged struggle with brain cancer. According to a statement shared by Maple Leaf Pro Wrestling, Doering passed away peacefull...

item.title: Critics report early operational problems at Trump-linked Great American State Fair
item.description: Three days into the 16-day Great American State Fair held on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., critics are already raising concerns about its execution and turnout. The event, organized by a grou...

item.title: Trump administration faces criticism after quietly expanding migrant labor access for dairy farms
item.description: The article reports that the Trump administration is facing criticism from anti-immigration activists after quietly issuing a Department of Labor memo that allows dairy farms to make greater use of mi...

item.title: Studio Fashion Photography in Vogue During the Late 1940s and Early 1950s
item.description: The article explores the evolution of Vogue studio photography during the late 1940s and early 1950s, a period often described as a golden age of fashion imagery. It highlights how postwar cultural sh...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos of Isabelle Adjani During the Filming of The Story of Adèle H. (1975)
item.description: The article presents a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs of Isabelle Adjani during the production of François Truffaut’s 1975 film “The Story of Adèle H.”, highlighting both her performance ...

item.title: JD Vance questioned on U.S. assessment of Iran nuclear program during interview with Bill Maher
item.description: Vice President JD Vance faced pointed questioning during a Friday appearance on HBO’s “Real Time with Bill Maher,” where the discussion centered on the Trump administration’s tentative peace deal with...

item.title: Lawsuit alleges police restraint and delayed medical care contributed to death of former NFL running back Doug Martin
item.description: The parents of former NFL running back Doug Martin have filed a wrongful death lawsuit claiming that police use of excessive force and delayed medical response contributed to his death in October of l...

item.title: Criticism grows over reported low attendance at Trump-linked Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.
item.description: The Great American State Fair, organized by the Trump-linked group Freedom 250 and held on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., has drawn significant public criticism following reports of unexpected...

item.title: Report: Growing conservative voter dissatisfaction with Trump tied to policy backlash and midterm concerns
item.description: A Raw Story report highlights growing dissatisfaction among some conservative voters toward former President Donald Trump during his second term, based on interviews cited by Reuters. According to the...

item.title: Mike Johnson Suggests GOP Would Shield Trump Allies From Investigations if Democrats Retake House
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson drew attention after remarks made at the Faith and Freedom Coalition’s annual summit, where he warned conservative attendees about the consequences of a potential Democratic...

item.title: House Oversight Democrat criticizes Leon Black after abrupt exit from Epstein-related interview
item.description: Billionaire investor Leon Black, former CEO of Apollo Global Management and a long-time associate of Jeffrey Epstein, faced strong criticism after abruptly leaving a closed-door interview with members...

item.title: Draft Board of Peace resolution proposes broad legal immunity for members overseeing Gaza reconstruction
item.description: A report by The Guardian claims that a draft resolution prepared by the Board of Peace, the international body established to oversee the reconstruction of Gaza, would grant extensive legal immunity t...

item.title: Annette Stroyberg: Danish Actress and European Cinema Icon of the Late 1950s and Early 1960s
item.description: Annette Stroyberg (1936–2005) was a Danish actress and model who became a prominent figure in European cinema during the late 1950s and early 1960s. She gained international attention after being disc...

item.title: New book excerpts raise questions about Donald Trump's reliance on aide Natalie Harp
item.description: A discussion on The Daily Beast Podcast focused on claims from the forthcoming book 'Regime Change' by reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan regarding the close working relationship between Pres...

item.title: Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Plus drops to $24.99 during Prime Day streaming deal
item.description: The article highlights a Prime Day promotion on Amazon’s Fire TV Stick lineup, focusing on the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus as the main deal. Originally priced around $49.99, the device is being offered at a...

item.title: Confederate Flag Removed from North Carolina Exhibit After Backlash at Great American State Fair
item.description: A display featuring Confederate flag imagery at the North Carolina exhibit during the Great American State Fair on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., sparked widespread criticism after a video of ...

item.title: CNN panel challenges MAGA commentator Caroline Sunshine over refugee policy claims during heated discussion
item.description: A CNN political panel moderated by anchor Abby Phillip became heated during a discussion involving former Trump White House staffer Caroline Sunshine, who defended Trump administration immigration and...

item.title: Texas lieutenant governor’s claim about separation of church and state sparks fact-checking backlash
item.description: During a public appearance at the White House on Friday, Texas Lieutenant Governor Dan Patrick sparked controversy after telling reporters that the phrase “separation of Church and state” is not found...

item.title: James Carville says future GOP primary candidates may attack Trump from within the MAGA movement
item.description: Veteran Democratic political strategist James Carville offered a prediction about the future direction of the Republican Party and how its presidential candidates may position themselves against Donal...

item.title: History of the NYPD Brooklyn Stand-Up Mugshot Identification System
item.description: The article explores the origins and development of the NYPD’s Brooklyn Stand-Up mugshot system, a photographic identification method introduced in 1918. Before this system was implemented, the New Yo...
